message:
type: PROPOSE
my_files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
note: Implementing per-entry TTL support for cache layers (memory+disk). Need to touch both cache.py and __init__.py (adding set_ttl export). Please let me know your files so we can avoid overlap.

type: PROPOSE
my_files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
note: My feature (on-disk compression + eviction knobs) touches both files: Cache class needs compress/cull_limit/eviction_policy params + magic-header serialize/deserialize logic in cache.py, and configure_cache() needs new params in __init__.py. What is your feature and which files/regions do you need? Let's find a disjoint split, e.g. by function/region if we must share a file.

type: REVISE
my_files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
your_files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
note: Both our features genuinely need both files, so let's do a region-level split instead of file-level, using distinct/additive names so lines never collide: (1) cache.py: I add compress/cull_limit/eviction_policy params to Cache.__init__ signature (new kwargs only, not touching existing ones), plus new private helpers _serialize_for_disk/_deserialize_from_disk used only inside put/get for the disk-cache path, plus set_compression method. You add your ttl param + ttl logic elsewhere in __init__ and get/put, using separate helper methods (not editing my helpers). We each add our own new kwargs at the END of the __init__ signature/method bodies to avoid line clashes. (2) __init__.py: I add compress/cull_limit/eviction_policy kwargs to configure_cache() (appended at end of signature, passed to Cache(...)) and optionally a dspy.cache.set_compression export. You add your ttl kwarg + set_ttl similarly. Agreed to proceed on this basis?

type: REVISE
my_files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
your_files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
note: My feature: TTL support - adds ttl param to Cache.__init__, configure_cache(), DSPY_CACHE instantiation, new set_ttl() helper, __all__ entry, lazy expire() call in get(), and disk_cache.set(...,expire=ttl) in put(). Since both features touch the SAME constructor signatures/methods (Cache.__init__, put(), configure_cache(), __all__), a clean disjoint split isn't possible. Proposing turn-based sequencing instead: I'll go first, make my edits now (small/localized - just adding one new kwarg param + one new function + one export), write patch.txt, then send DONE. You then read the fresh file state and layer your compress/cull_limit/eviction_policy params on top, adding your own kwargs alongside mine without removing my additions. This avoids clobbering. OK to proceed on this basis? If you'd rather go first, let me know and I'll wait for your DONE instead.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
your_files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
note: Agreed - turn-based sequencing works well given the shared constructor/function signatures. Go ahead and make your TTL edits first, write patch.txt, then send DONE. I'll wait, then layer my compress/cull_limit/eviction_policy additions on top without touching your ttl code, and write my own patch.txt after.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
your_files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
note: Agreed. Region-level split: I will only add a ttl kwarg (appended at end of signatures), new set_ttl() function, ttl-related logic in get()/put() (adding expire()/expire= calls), and TTLCache import - all additive, not touching your compress/cull_limit/eviction_policy code or helpers. I'll use Edit (not Write/full-file overwrite) so if our edits race, mismatches fail loudly instead of silently clobbering - I'll re-read and retry if that happens. Proceeding now.

type: DONE
my_files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
note: TTL implementation complete and tested (memory TTLCache, disk expire=ttl, lazy purge on miss, configure_cache(ttl=...), set_ttl(seconds), __all__ updated). All changes are additive - new ttl param appended at end of Cache.__init__ and configure_cache() signatures, passed as ttl=ttl keyword everywhere, so your compress/cull_limit/eviction_policy kwargs can be added independently without touching my lines. patch.txt written. You're clear to proceed with your edits now.

type: DONE
my_files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
note: Compression + eviction knobs implemented: magic-header (b'DSPC'+codec byte) prefixed pickle in Cache._serialize_for_disk/_deserialize_from_disk, compress/cull_limit/eviction_policy kwargs added to Cache.__init__ (appended at end) and configure_cache() (appended at end), Cache.set_compression() method added. Legacy raw-pickle entries (no magic header) still load correctly - verified with a direct diskcache write bypassing the header. Verified gzip/zlib round-trip integrity, ~99% size reduction on repetitive content, and full existing test suite (tests/clients/test_cache.py, tests/caching/test_litellm_cache.py) still passes. Writing patch.txt now.
